summary: Tests cover all major requirements (glob patterns, multi-label assignment, priority ordering, error handling, end-to-end workflow) with good logical organization and proper Pester structure. However, rigor is limited by missing edge case coverage (case sensitivity, whitespace, pattern boundaries, degenerate inputs). Design is hindered by fragile regex-based output parsing from act logs and complex BeforeDiscovery/BeforeAll patterns. Critical concern: fixture files (fixtures/case-*.json) are referenced but not shown, and their exact structure/content is undocumented, making test reliability questionable. Would catch most regressions but miss edge cases and has brittleness in integration test parsing.
